prevalence.trend: increasing with aging and diabetes prevalence

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to pain, gait impairment and falls

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical neuropathic symptoms and exam; nerve conduction studies when available; evaluate reversible causes

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: opportunistic screening in diabetes care; no population screening
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; depends on etiology control and pain management

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care; EMG/NCS in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Treat underlying cause, glycemic control, neuropathic pain meds (duloxetine, pregabalin), physiotherapy and fall prevention
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to EMG and specialist neurology in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Core medications and diabetes programs available in SUS
